commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens Claude, Kiro, and MiniMax plan parsing regexes so plan labels cannot capture across rendered newline boundaries, and adds Claude regression coverage for the `/usage` toggle-hint leak.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has newline-capable `\s` in the Claude plan phrase regex, and the PR body plus inspected screenshots show the visible `Dtoday` failure and after-fix behavior; I avoided live provider probes per repository policy.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed because the PR is already a narrow implementation candidate and has no actionable review findings.

target.title: fix: stop plan regex from bleeding across TUI lines
target.body: ## Summary Fixes the Claude menu card showing a garbage plan label (e.g. **"Dtoday"** instead of **"Pro"**). The plan/login extraction regexes used `\s`, which matches newlines, inside their capture. On an ANSI-stripped TUI capture a match could bridge from one rendered line into unrelated text on the next. For Claude, stripping the cursor-positioning escapes glued "…accumulates as you use Claude" onto the `/usage` panel's "d → today · w → week" toggle hint ("dtoday"), so `claude\s+…` matched `"Claude\n\ndtoday"` → `"Dtoday"`. Reproduced against live `claude` 2.1.170 output. ## Implementation - Restrict the Claude plan-phrase regex in `ClaudeStatusProbe` to horizontal whitespace (`[ \t]`) so a match stays on a single rendered line. - Audit every provider for the same construct. Three shared it; the rest are safe (captures excluding `\s`, line-bounded `.+`/`[^\n]`, `@`-required emails, or intentional lazy `[\s\S]`). - Apply the same hardening defensively to the other two: MiniMax `Coding Plan` and Kiro `| KIRO `. A real single-line plan phrase still matches. - Add regression tests covering the `/usage` toggle-hint text (no longer yields `"Dtoday"`) and single-line plan extraction (`Claude Max` → `Max`). ## Verification - `swift build` clean. - 41 Claude tests pass, including the two new regression tests. - 143 MiniMax/Kiro tests pass — defensive changes don't regress their plan parsing. - Root cause confirmed by replaying the extraction over a live `claude` 2.1.170 `/usage` capture: old regex produced `"Dtoday"`, new regex returns `nil` (CLI falls back to OAuth's correct `"Pro"`). ## Screenshots Before: After:
target.author: elijahfriedman
